summary: End-to-end testing of AYANA-BOT iteration 2 (plans + conversational templates + WhatsApp LIVE). Backend: 30/30 pytest tests pass — /config returns plans (basic 149 INR / care_plus 399 INR), currencies (INR/USD/GBP/EUR/AED/SGD/AUD/CAD), categories with type tags, and whatsapp_enabled=true. Plan-based limits enforced: Basic rejects 4 check-ins (400 with 'up to 3'), rejects 3 reminders (400 with 'up to 2'), accepts 3+2; Care+ accepts 10 check-ins. Payment checkout stores {plan,billing} correctly and defaults unknown plans to basic. Conversational preview endpoint returns casual Telugu ('అమ్మా') + reply footer ('👉 రిప్లై') and Hindi/English footers with '👉' arrow, done/not-yet options. Admin & account flows intact. Frontend: signup→onboarding→basic plan pick→schedule editor→activation→dashboard flow completed. Landing pricing billing toggle (month↔year) and currency dropdown (INR↔USD) update prices correctly (₹149/mo, ₹1430/yr, $1.99/mo, $19/yr). Onboarding step order matches spec (child, parent, plan pick, schedule, activate). Schedule editor UI correctly caps Basic at 3 checkins + 2 reminders with add buttons disabled at limit. Dashboard edit-schedule dialog uses the grouped ScheduleEditor and respects plan limits. shadcn Select for category renders 8 options in a portal without dialog clipping. Admin dashboard shows 'WhatsApp: Live' badge and Users/Deliveries/Emergencies tabs.

ui_bugs.issue: Phone field is a plain <input> with placeholder '+919876543210' — the new PhoneInput country-code dropdown component is NOT used here. Review request explicitly asked: 'Signup with country-code dropdown phone (+91 default) works'. No data-testid='signup-phone-code' exists. Users must type the full E.164 string manually.

issues: Plan badge ('AYANA Basic · Trial') has no data-testid; consider adding data-testid='plan-badge' for automated tests.
Radix DialogContent emits console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}' — add DialogDescription for a11y.

action_items: Wire the <PhoneInput> component into /signup (replace the plain phone <input>) so E.164 is auto-built from country code + digits, matching the child/parent phone inputs elsewhere. Add data-testid='signup-phone-code' via the PhoneInput testid prop.
Add data-testid='plan-badge' to the header plan chip in Dashboard.js (line ~75) for future automated tests.
Add a DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to the Add/Edit schedule and Add/Edit parent dialogs to silence Radix a11y warnings.

Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md — it still reports WHATSAPP_ENABLED=false but backend/.env now has WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true (Twilio sandbox); align docs with reality to avoid confusing next agents.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/models.py ScheduleInput.limit_messages is still a no-op field_validator (returns v as-is); actual enforcement lives in server._validate_by_plan. Move to model_validator or drop the dead validator.

backend/server.py PUT /parents/{parent_id} still lacks a `deleted_at: None` filter on find_one — a soft-deleted parent can be resurrected via PUT. Low risk since UI never surfaces deleted parents, but worth tightening.

backend/server.py /messages/preview: ignored `custom_text` gets rendered without variant selection when the template exists AND custom_text is provided (custom_text wins). Good, but no path currently forces a non-default `day_index`; consider seeding a stable rotation key so users don't see the same variant twice in a row.

backend/whatsapp.py: on Twilio failure, send_whatsapp returns {status: 'failed', detail: <str(exc)>} which is fine, but activate() surfaces this to the client as-is. For UX, consider grouping failures by recipient and providing an actionable message (e.g., 'Not joined to sandbox yet').
backend/.env: WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true with TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN committed in plaintext. Rotate this token after testing; consider moving to a secret manager.
CORS still uses allow_origins=['*'] + allow_credentials=True (invalid combination). Since the app uses Bearer tokens it works, but is a config smell.
Frontend Signup.js: does NOT use PhoneInput (see ui_bug above). All other phone fields (child-phone, parent-phone, pd-phone, sd-phone if present) correctly use PhoneInput with -code testid.

Frontend ScheduleEditor: add-reminder always defaults new rows to 'medicine' (firstOfType), so two consecutive reminder chips render as 'Medicine Reminder' until user changes the category. Minor UX: consider cycling defaults or defaulting the second reminder to 'water' since Basic plan allows only 2.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_ayana_api.py (rewrote for new plan-based logic, WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true, conversational reply footer, /payment/checkout payload)

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (full E2E flow works; signup PhoneInput is the only miss)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@ayana.care / AyanaAdmin@2026. Test user created during run: e2e_u18ygrnm@example.com / test1234 (Basic plan, activated). New signups must use E.164 phones like +919876543210 (@example.com emails only; pydantic EmailStr rejects .test/.local).
seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_-prefixed users/parents/schedules created via pytest and Playwright runs. e2e_* prefix used for browser flow user. No cleanup performed but names are easily identifiable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_ayana_api.py (30 tests, xdist 2 workers, ~7s). Fixtures: `fresh_user` gives a brand-new registered user with `.headers`; `auth_headers` reuses a session-scoped user. WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true so /activation/activate returns real Twilio results — status='failed' for numbers not joined to the sandbox is EXPECTED. Frontend flow: /signup → /onboarding steps 0..4 (child, parent, plan via PricingCards select-plan-basic/care_plus, schedule via ScheduleEditor, activation) → /dashboard. All phone fields except signup use <PhoneInput>. If the previous agent adds PhoneInput to Signup, the flow test can just fill signup-phone-code (via select_option '+91') + signup-phone (digits only).
rca of the issue: Signup PhoneInput miss: the review request lists signup PhoneInput as an iteration goal, but /app/frontend/src/pages/Signup.js (lines 78-82) still renders a plain <input data-testid='signup-phone'>. All other phone inputs (Onboarding child/parent, Dashboard pd-phone, sd-phone) were correctly migrated to <PhoneInput>. Fix: replace the plain <input> block with `<PhoneInput value={form.phone} onChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, phone: v })} testid="signup-phone" />` and drop the `+` from the placeholder note. Backend RegisterInput.clean_phone already validates E.164, so PhoneInput's `${dial}${digits}` output remains compatible.
